reasoning: The informal statement asserts that A × B is an abelian group iff both A and B are abelian. Talking about "abelian" presupposes a group structure, so the `[Group A] [Group B]` assumptions are implicit premises. Given those, A × B automatically has a group structure in Mathlib, and "A × B is abelian" reduces precisely to `∀ x y : A × B, x * y = y * x`, which is what the Lean statement uses. The right-hand side similarly encodes "A abelian ∧ B abelian" as commutativity of the respective group operations. The biconditional and quantifier structure match the informal claim exactly, so this is a faithful formalization.
